name: Jak Silver
bio:
Physical Appearance: Imperial male. Wears a plaid shirt, black wide pants, and leather boots. Armed with a steel cutlass. Carries a cheese wedge.
Race: Imperial
Personality Keywords: entrepreneurial, skilled, piratical, supplier, eloquent
Role: A nightblade merchant aboard the Black Flag who serves as the supplier for Dunbarrow Cove once the upgrade is purchased from Dahlia Rackham for 1000 gold. He sells potions and lockpicks to all, including unique Nightmask potions. For customers with Mercantile skill of 50 or higher, he also offers weapons and armor, including enchanted versions. He provides advanced Speechcraft training, teaching customers the art of persuasion—a useful skill for both merchants and pirates.
Home Location Information: Belowdecks on the Black Flag at Dunbarrow Cove, where he sleeps on an empty bedroll and conducts his business.
Daily Routine: Jak sleeps from 2am to 10am on a bedroll inside the Black Flag. He is available to barter from 10am until 11pm, when he closes shop and has a three-hour supper before going to bed.
Personality - The Pirate Merchant: Jak Silver represents the entrepreneurial spirit of piracy—he's not just a thief but a businessman who understands that a successful pirate crew needs reliable supplies and a smooth-talking quartermaster. His advanced Speechcraft training suggests someone who has talked his way out of (and into) countless situations, understanding that words can be as valuable as weapons in the pirate trade. His requirement that customers demonstrate decent Mercantile skill before accessing his best wares shows shrewd business sense—he only does serious deals with those who understand trade. The unique Nightmask potions he offers hint at connections to shadowy suppliers or knowledge of rare formulas. He's adapted to the pirate life aboard the Black Flag, sleeping on a simple bedroll despite his profitable trade, suggesting either contentment with simplicity or recognition that pirates must be ready to move quickly. His cutlass and nightblade training indicate he's no helpless merchant—he can defend his wares and himself when words fail.
author: Bethesda
